title: Smoky Venezuelan Tofu Bowl
description: A resinous, light monsoon inspired bowl that blends smoky binchotan charcoal, subtle rum, and fresh Venezuelan flavors in a vegan-friendly dish.
instructions: Press tofu for 20 minutes to remove excess moisture and then cube it.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and sear tofu cubes until golden on all sides, about 5 minutes.
In a small bowl whisk together rum, soy sauce, binchotan charcoal powder, and a pinch of sea salt, then pour over tofu and cook for 2 minutes while stirring.
Meanwhile, rinse quinoa under cold water, then combine with 2 cups water or broth and bring to a boil; reduce heat, cover, and simmer until liquid is absorbed, about 15 minutes.
Fluff quinoa with a fork and let it rest for 5 minutes before transferring to bowls.
Distribute tofu over quinoa and top with sliced avocado, shredded cabbage, and toasted sesame seeds.
Squeeze lime wedges over the bowl and garnish with extra sesame seeds if desired.
